text.title: Data Modeling Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how data-modeling produces a valid spec artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- artefact_id: schema-orders-2026-05-23 owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> engine: postgres 16 ---
## Pass 1 — Conceptual - Customer 1--* Order *--* LineItem >--1 Item
## Pass 2 — Logical | Entity | Attrs | Keys | |--------|-------|------| | Order | id, customer_id, status, total, created_at | PK(id), FK(customer_id) | | LineItem | order_id, item_id, qty, unit_price | PK(order_id, item_id), FK(order_id), FK(item_id) |
## Pass 3 — Physical ```sql CREATE TABLE orders ( id uuid PRIMARY KEY, customer_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(id), status order_status NOT NULL, total numeric(12,2) NOT NULL, created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now() ); CREATE INDEX orders_customer_created ON orders (customer_id, created_at DESC); ```
## Migration plan - DDL: migrations/2026-05-23-orders.sql - Rollback: drop tables + restore from _backup_2026_05_22.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
